text.title: langchain-observability Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for instrumenting LangChain chains with LangSmith, structured callbacks, redaction, sampling, and streaming.

rule.statement: Enable LangSmith tracing from day one of production. Retrofitting tracing after an incident captures none of the data needed to debug it.
rule.rationale: Intermediate steps, token usage, and tool calls are only captured during the live run. Without tracing the only debug surface is application logs, which miss the LLM step entirely.

rule.statement: Assert LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true at app boot in production; refuse to start the service if missing.
rule.rationale: A silent off-switch on tracing has historically led to long outages where 'we have no idea what happened'. Treat it as required infrastructure.

rule.statement: When pii_posture is internal or no-export, attach a redaction callback BEFORE the LangSmith handler.
rule.rationale: Trust no upstream sanitization; the call site is the only place you can guarantee redaction. SSNs and email addresses must never reach the trace UI.

rule.statement: Override on_chain_error in any custom CallbackHandler with raise_error=True or a dead-letter push. Default behavior swallows exceptions.
rule.rationale: BaseCallbackHandler swallows callback exceptions by design to protect chains; this hides bugs in observability code itself.

rule.statement: For production sampling, either trace at 1.0 for the first 30 days, or use tail-based sampling that keeps every run with status=error.
rule.rationale: Uniform low-rate sampling misses the rare failures you most need to see. Either trace everything early, or sample by outcome.

rule.statement: Enable streaming when latency_target_ms ≤ 500. The UX cliff falls fast below 500ms perceived latency.
rule.rationale: Streaming first-token latency dominates user perception; below 500ms users perceive instant response.

rule.statement: Set LANGCHAIN_PROJECT per deployment environment (dev/staging/prod); never share a single project across envs.
rule.rationale: Shared projects mix noisy dev traffic with prod, hiding real incidents.

rule.statement: For pii_posture=no-export, fall back to opentelemetry to a local collector; never use the hosted LangSmith endpoint.
rule.rationale: Regulated workloads cannot leak payloads to a third-party SaaS, even for debugging.
